# AxiomID — Full Documentation

> W3C DID-based identity layer for humans delegating authority to AI agents on Pi Network and Stellar.

## Overview

AxiomID is a decentralized identity system that allows humans to delegate authority to AI agents using W3C DID standards, Ed25519 cryptographic keys, and Verifiable Credentials.

### Architecture

The system consists of:
- **Frontend**: Next.js 16 App Router with Tailwind CSS
- **Backend**: Cloudflare Workers (Edge) + Vercel Functions
- **Database**: PostgreSQL (Prisma ORM) + Cloudflare D1 (SQLite)
- **Blockchain**: Stellar (DID anchoring) + Pi Network (KYC/identity)
- **Crypto**: Ed25519 keys (jose library), HMAC-SHA256 derivation

### Identity Tiers

| Tier | XP Required | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| Visitor | 0 | Basic access, rate-limited |
| Citizen | 100 | Pi KYC verified, basic skills |
| Validator | 500 | Community verified, advanced skills |
| Sovereign | 1000 | Full delegation authority, premium skills |

## API Reference

### Public Endpoints (No Auth Required)

#### GET /api
API root discovery endpoint. Returns list of all available endpoints.

#### GET /api/did-document
W3C DID document resolution. Returns JSON-LD DID document.
- Query param: `?did=did:axiom:username` (optional, returns issuer DID if omitted)
- Content-Type: `application/did+ld+json`
- Cache: `public, max-age=86400, stale-while-revalidate=3600`

#### GET /.well-known/jwks.json
JSON Web Key Set. Returns Ed25519 public keys for signature verification.
- Cache: `public, s-maxage=3600, stale-while-revalidate=86400`

#### GET /auth.md
Protocol discovery document. Returns markdown with API documentation.

### Protected Endpoints (Auth Required)

#### POST /api/agent/identity
Verify Pi ID-JAG token and create/update agent identity.
- Body: `{ idToken: string }`
- Returns: `{ user, agent, did }`

#### POST /api/agent/sign
Sign payload with sovereign Ed25519 key.
- Body: `{ payload: string, did: string }`
- Returns: `{ signature: string, kid: string }`

#### GET /api/agent/manifest
Verifiable Credential manifest. Returns available VC types.

#### POST /api/oauth2/token
JWT bearer grant exchange. Convert Pi token to AxiomID access token.
- Body: `{ grant_type: "urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer", assertion: string }`
- Returns: `{ access_token, token_type, expires_in }`

#### POST /api/oauth2/revoke
Revoke access or refresh token.
- Body: `{ token: string, token_type_hint?: "access_token" | "refresh_token" }`

### Marketplace Endpoints

#### GET /api/skills
Search and list available skills.
- Query: `?q=search&tag=tag&sort=price`

#### GET /api/skills/tags
List all skill categories.

#### POST /api/skills/[skillId]/purchase
Purchase a skill with Pi Coin.
- Body: `{ paymentId: string }`

#### GET /api/agent/skills
List skills owned by authenticated agent.

## DID Document Format

```json
{
  "@context": ["https://www.w3.org/ns/did/v1", "https://w3id.org/security/suites/ed25519-2020/v1"],
  "id": "did:axiom:piUsername",
  "controller": "did:axiom:axiom-root",
  "verificationMethod": [{
    "id": "did:axiom:piUsername#key-1",
    "type": "Ed25519VerificationKey2020",
    "controller": "did:axiom:piUsername",
    "publicKeyMultibase": "z..."
  }],
  "authentication": ["did:axiom:piUsername#key-1"],
  "capabilityDelegation": ["did:axiom:piUsername#key-1"],
  "service": [{
    "id": "did:axiom:piUsername#agent",
    "type": "AgentService",
    "serviceEndpoint": "https://axiomid.app/api/agent"
  }]
}
```

## Error Responses

All API errors follow this envelope:
```json
{
  "error": "Error message",
  "code": "ERROR_CODE"
}
```

Error codes: `VALIDATION_ERROR`, `NOT_FOUND`, `RATE_LIMITED`, `INTERNAL_ERROR`, `UNAUTHORIZED`, `FORBIDDEN`

## Authentication

1. User connects Pi wallet in Pi Browser
2. Pi SDK returns ID-JAG token
3. POST to `/api/agent/identity` with token
4. Receive AxiomID access token
5. Use token in `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header

## Rate Limits

- Public endpoints: 60 requests/minute per IP
- Authenticated endpoints: 120 requests/minute per user
- Signing endpoints: 10 requests/minute per user

## For AI Agents

### Quick Start
```bash
# 1. Get API catalog
curl https://axiomid.app/api

# 2. Get DID document
curl https://axiomid.app/api/did-document?did=did:axiom:username

# 3. Verify signature
curl https://axiomid.app/.well-known/jwks.json
```

### DID Format
- Format: `did:axiom:piUsername`
- Example: `did:axiom:Pioneer.Axiom`

### Signing
- Algorithm: Ed25519
- Library: jose (RFC 8037)
- Key derivation: HMAC-SHA256 with SOVEREIGN_KEY_SALT

### Sandbox Mode
Add header: `X-Sandbox: true`

## Security

- Private keys never stored — derived deterministically
- All auth endpoints rate-limited
- Zod validation at all trust boundaries
- Token revocation supported
- W3C DID Document for key rotation

## Links

- Website: https://axiomid.app
- Documentation: https://axiomid.app/docs
- GitHub: https://github.com/Moeabdelaziz007/AxiomID
- DID Resolution: https://axiomid.app/api/did-document
- JWKS: https://axiomid.app/.well-known/jwks.json
